config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat dan kebebasan setiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi agar tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat boleh dibantu, tetapi hak dasar individu tetap harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only when those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If we flip the hierarchy and make society the default priority, it becomes far too easy to trample minorities and unpopular people in the name of the majority. The better principle is that a healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first, then balancing them carefully when there is a real and specific public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak adil, apalagi ketika yang terdampak justru kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial itu penting, tetapi bagi saya posisinya bukan di atas hak dasar; yang benar adalah negara boleh membatasi secara sangat terbatas, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dibuat nomor dua, yang paling mudah kalah biasanya bukan masyarakat secara keseluruhan, melainkan warga biasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify majoritarian abuse, racial discrimination, and government overreach. Public needs absolutely matter, but they should only limit individual rights in narrow, clearly defined, and accountable ways, not as a default rule. When society is treated as more important than the person, the people who usually pay the price are minorities, dissidents, and anyone without much power. To me, a free society is strongest when individual rights are the starting point and any exception has to be carefully justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga biasa, terutama minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya akses kuasa. Saya setuju masyarakat butuh batasan tertentu untuk hal seperti keselamatan publik, tetapi batas itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengalahkan hak dasar. Kalau hak individu ditempatkan di bawah masyarakat secara otomatis, yang sering terjadi bukan masyarakat jadi lebih adil, melainkan yang lemah makin mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, that framing has too often been used to excuse discrimination, forced conformity, and government overreach, and I do not trust it as a default rule. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should only justify limited exceptions when the need is real, specific, and tightly accountable. If the burden of proof is not on society to justify restricting rights, then the people most likely to lose are minorities and ordinary citizens with the least power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
